---
title: "QT_Model_Ayman"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
editor: visual
---

```{r, include = FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      echo    = FALSE)

```

```{r, message = FALSE}

library(tidyverse)
library(tidyquant)

```

# Section 1 — Data

```{r}

# Pull daily OHLCV for all sector ETFs, SPY (benchmark), and XLG (performance tracker)
# Training: 2000-06-01 – 2024-12-31 | Test: 2025-01-01 – 2026-03-31
# Note: XLC (2018), XLG (2005), XLRE (2015) have shorter histories — handled via dynamic universe
df <- tidyquant::tq_get(
  c("SPY", "XLB", "XLC", "XLE", "XLF", "XLG", "XLI", "XLK", "XLP", "XLRE", "XLV", "XLU", "XLY"),
  get  = "stock.prices",
  from = "2000-06-01",
  to   = "2026-03-31"
)

```

## Data Coverage

```{r}

# Confirm date range and observation count per ticker
df %>%
  group_by(symbol) %>%
  summarise(
    first_date = min(date),
    last_date  = max(date),
    n_obs      = n(),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(first_date)

```

## Data Quality — NA / NaN / NULL

```{r}

# NA count per column per symbol
# NaN in R is a special case of NA for numeric columns — is.na() catches both
na_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(symbol) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.))), .groups = "drop")

na_summary

```

```{r}

# Flag any rows where price columns are zero or negative — indicates data corruption
df %>%
  filter(open <= 0 | high <= 0 | low <= 0 | close <= 0 | adjusted <= 0) %>%
  nrow() %>%
  {cat("Rows with zero or negative prices:", ., "\n")}

```

```{r}

# Check for missing trading dates: any date where some symbols have data but others don't
# A clean dataset should have the same set of dates for all symbols active at that point
date_coverage <- df %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(n_symbols = n_distinct(symbol), .groups = "drop")

cat("Expected symbols per date (full universe):", n_distinct(df$symbol), "\n")
cat("Dates with fewer symbols than expected:", sum(date_coverage$n_symbols < n_distinct(df$symbol)), "\n")

```

## Return Computation

```{r}

# Yahoo Finance only provides adjusted close, not adjusted open.
# Derive adjusted open using the daily adjustment ratio: adj_open = open * (adjusted / close)
# This applies the same split/dividend scaling to open prices for consistency.
#
# Return conventions (all log returns — additive across time, standard for PCA and analysis):
#   cc_return: close-to-close (primary return for all analysis, PCA, and feature computation)
#   oo_return: open-to-open (adjusted)
#   co_return: overnight gap — today's adj open vs yesterday's adj close
#   oc_return: intraday — today's adj close vs today's adj open
#
# Execution convention for portfolio simulation (Section 6) is tentative — determined by
# execution logic. Arithmetic returns will be derived from whichever convention is chosen.
df <- df %>%
  group_by(symbol) %>%
  arrange(date) %>%
  mutate(
    adj_open  = open * (adjusted / close),
    cc_return = log(adjusted / lag(adjusted)),
    oo_return = log(adj_open  / lag(adj_open)),
    co_return = log(adj_open  / lag(adjusted)),
    oc_return = log(adjusted  / adj_open)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

```

```{r}

# Sanity check — quantiles across all return conventions to catch data corruption or outliers
df %>%
  filter(!is.na(cc_return)) %>%
  select(symbol, cc_return, oo_return, co_return, oc_return) %>%
  pivot_longer(-symbol, names_to = "convention", values_to = "return") %>%
  group_by(convention) %>%
  summarise(
    min    = round(min(return,                 na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    q01    = round(quantile(return, 0.01,      na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    median = round(median(return,              na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    q99    = round(quantile(return, 0.99,      na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    max    = round(max(return,                 na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```

## Price History

```{r, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6}

# Adjusted closing price over full history — confirms data loaded correctly
# SPY and XLG shown separately as benchmarks
df %>%
  mutate(role = case_when(
    symbol %in% c("SPY", "XLG") ~ "Benchmark",
    TRUE                        ~ "Sector ETF"
  )) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = date, y = adjusted, colour = symbol)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(~role, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title  = "Adjusted Closing Prices — Full History",
    x      = NULL,
    y      = "Adjusted Close ($)",
    colour = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

# Section 2 — Algo Dataset Construction

```{r}

# Build the canonical algo dataset from df
# Selects only columns needed for signal computation and portfolio simulation
# SPY and XLG are retained — excluded from clustering/features in later sections, not here
# Rolling return columns (over lookback_days) added after Section 3 locks the parameter
#
# Columns:
#   date, symbol  — identity
#   open          — adjusted open  (adj_open: split/dividend-scaled for consistency with close)
#   close         — adjusted close (Yahoo's adjusted field)
#   cc_return     — log close-to-close (primary return: PCA, features, signal computation)
#   oo_return     — log open-to-open (adjusted)
#   co_return     — log overnight gap (adj open today vs adj close yesterday)
#   oc_return     — log intraday (adj close vs adj open today)
algo_df <- df %>%
  select(date, symbol,
         open  = adj_open,
         close = adjusted,
         cc_return, oo_return, co_return, oc_return)

glimpse(algo_df)

# Split into training and test sets — defined once here, referenced throughout
# Training: 2000-06-01 – 2024-12-31 (model fitting, PCA, clustering, feature calibration)
# Test:     2025-01-01 – 2026-03-31 (signal simulation and evaluation only — no parameter fitting)
train_df <- algo_df %>% filter(date <= as.Date("2024-12-31"))
test_df  <- algo_df %>% filter(date >= as.Date("2025-01-01") & date <= as.Date("2026-03-31"))

```

# Section 3 — Lookback Period Selection

```{r, message = FALSE}

library(gt)

```

```{r}

# Fixed holding period — monthly rebalancing throughout
# Candidates: 1, 3, 6, 12 months expressed in trading days
H            <- 21L
L_candidates <- c(21L, 63L, 126L, 252L)

# Project color palette — shared across all gt tables in this document
col_navy        <- "#1B2A4A"
col_slate       <- "#4A6FA5"
col_light_slate <- "#D6E4F0"
col_band        <- "#F5F7FA"

```

```{r}

# For one ETF and one L candidate:
#   1. Build two-column table (signal, forward_return) with non-overlapping holding periods
#   2. Signal:  mean cc_return over past L days at each step point
#   3. Forward: sum cc_return over next H days at each step point
#   4. Stride = H because all L >= H (Chan 2013 — non-overlapping holding periods)
#   5. Run cor.test and return one summary row
#
# Example: compute_lookback_cor("XLK", 63L, 21L, train_df)
compute_lookback_cor <- function(sym, L, H, data) {

  returns <- data %>%
    filter(symbol == sym) %>%
    arrange(date) %>%
    pull(cc_return)

  # Drop leading NA (first obs per symbol has no lag)
  returns <- returns[!is.na(returns)]
  n       <- length(returns)

  # Step points: i has L days behind it and H days ahead
  step_pts <- seq(from = L + 1, to = n - H + 1, by = H)
  if (length(step_pts) < 10) return(NULL)

  signal  <- map_dbl(step_pts, ~ mean(returns[(.x - L):(.x - 1)]))
  forward <- map_dbl(step_pts, ~ sum(returns[.x:(.x + H - 1)]))

  ct <- cor.test(signal, forward, method = "pearson")

  tibble(
    symbol  = sym,
    L       = L,
    H       = H,
    n_obs   = length(step_pts),
    r       = round(as.numeric(ct$estimate), 4),
    r_sq    = round(as.numeric(ct$estimate)^2, 4),
    p_value = round(ct$p.value, 4)
  )
}

```

```{r}

# Run across all symbols x all L candidates — one row per ETF x L in output
symbols_universe <- unique(train_df$symbol)

lookback_results <- map_dfr(symbols_universe, function(sym) {
  map_dfr(L_candidates, function(L) {
    compute_lookback_cor(sym, L, H, train_df)
  })
})

```

```{r}

# Aggregate to one row per L: median r, median r_sq, and proportion of ETFs with p < 0.05
lookback_summary <- lookback_results %>%
  group_by(L, H) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_n_obs       = round(mean(n_obs)),
    median_r        = round(median(r), 3),
    median_r_sq     = round(median(r_sq), 3),
    pct_significant = mean(p_value < 0.05),
    .groups         = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(L)

# Selection: highest median_r among candidates where majority of ETFs are significant
# Prefer shorter L on ties
selected_L <- lookback_summary %>%
  filter(pct_significant >= 0.5) %>%
  arrange(desc(median_r), L) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  pull(L)

# Fallback: if no candidate clears the threshold, pick highest pct_significant
if (length(selected_L) == 0) {
  selected_L <- lookback_summary %>%
    arrange(desc(pct_significant), desc(median_r)) %>%
    slice(1) %>%
    pull(L)
  warning("No lookback candidate cleared the 50% significance threshold — selected by highest pct_significant.")
}

lookback_days <- selected_L
cat("Selected lookback_days:", lookback_days, "\n")

```

```{r}

# Diagnostic table — one row per L candidate
# Significant rows (majority of ETFs p < 0.05) highlighted in slate-blue
# Selected row bolded
lookback_summary %>%
  mutate(selected = if_else(L == lookback_days, "Yes", "")) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title    = "Lookback Period Selection",
    subtitle = "Non-overlapping correlation: lookback signal vs 21-day forward return | Training data only"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    L               = "Lookback (days)",
    H               = "Holding (days)",
    avg_n_obs       = "Avg Samples",
    median_r        = "Median r",
    median_r_sq     = "Median r²",
    pct_significant = "% ETFs Significant",
    selected        = "Selected"
  ) %>%
  fmt_percent(columns = pct_significant, decimals = 0) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(median_r, median_r_sq), decimals = 3) %>%
  # Dark navy header
  tab_style(
    style     = list(cell_fill(color = col_navy), cell_text(color = "white", weight = "bold")),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  # Alternating row bands
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_fill(color = col_band),
    locations = cells_body(rows = c(1, 3))
  ) %>%
  # Highlight selected row
  tab_style(
    style     = list(cell_fill(color = col_light_slate), cell_text(weight = "bold")),
    locations = cells_body(rows = selected == "Yes")
  ) %>%
  # Title color
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(color = col_navy, weight = "bold", size = "large"),
    locations = cells_title(groups = "title")
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(color = col_slate, size = "small"),
    locations = cells_title(groups = "subtitle")
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.border.top.color            = col_navy,
    table.border.bottom.color         = col_navy,
    column_labels.border.bottom.color = col_navy,
    table.font.size                   = 14,
    data_row.padding                  = px(6)
  )

```

```{r}

# Detail table — one row per ETF x L, ordered by L then symbol
# Significant rows (p < 0.05) highlighted in slate-blue
# Fixed height showing ~6-7 rows; remainder scrollable
lookback_results %>%
  arrange(L, symbol) %>%
  mutate(significant = p_value < 0.05) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title    = "Lookback Correlation — Full Detail",
    subtitle = "One row per ETF × lookback candidate | Training data only"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    symbol      = "ETF",
    L           = "Lookback (days)",
    H           = "Holding (days)",
    n_obs       = "Samples",
    r           = "r",
    r_sq        = "r²",
    p_value     = "p-value",
    significant = "p < 0.05"
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(r, r_sq, p_value), decimals = 3) %>%
  cols_hide(significant) %>%
  # Navy header
  tab_style(
    style     = list(cell_fill(color = col_navy), cell_text(color = "white", weight = "bold")),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  # Highlight significant rows
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_fill(color = col_light_slate),
    locations = cells_body(rows = significant == TRUE)
  ) %>%
  # Title styling
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(color = col_navy, weight = "bold", size = "large"),
    locations = cells_title(groups = "title")
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(color = col_slate, size = "small"),
    locations = cells_title(groups = "subtitle")
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.border.top.color            = col_navy,
    table.border.bottom.color         = col_navy,
    column_labels.border.bottom.color = col_navy,
    table.font.size                   = 14,
    data_row.padding                  = px(6),
    container.height                  = px(220),
    container.overflow.y              = "auto"
  )

```

```{r}

# Pull SPY returns as a named vector (date → cc_return) for fast lookup
spy_returns <- train_df %>%
  filter(symbol == "SPY") %>%
  arrange(date) %>%
  select(date, spy_return = cc_return)

# Relative return version: signal and forward both use ETF cc_return minus SPY cc_return
# Same non-overlapping stride logic as absolute version (stride = H, all L >= H)
# SPY excluded — cannot compute relative return for SPY vs itself
#
# Example: compute_rel_cor("XLK", 63L, 21L, train_df, spy_returns)
compute_rel_cor <- function(sym, L, H, data, spy_ret) {

  etf <- data %>%
    filter(symbol == sym) %>%
    arrange(date) %>%
    select(date, cc_return)

  # Join SPY return by date and compute excess return
  joined <- etf %>%
    inner_join(spy_ret, by = "date") %>%
    mutate(excess = cc_return - spy_return)

  returns <- joined %>% pull(excess)
  returns <- returns[!is.na(returns)]
  n       <- length(returns)

  step_pts <- seq(from = L + 1, to = n - H + 1, by = H)
  if (length(step_pts) < 10) return(NULL)

  signal  <- map_dbl(step_pts, ~ mean(returns[(.x - L):(.x - 1)]))
  forward <- map_dbl(step_pts, ~ sum(returns[.x:(.x + H - 1)]))

  ct <- cor.test(signal, forward, method = "pearson")

  tibble(
    symbol  = sym,
    L       = L,
    H       = H,
    n_obs   = length(step_pts),
    r       = round(as.numeric(ct$estimate), 4),
    r_sq    = round(as.numeric(ct$estimate)^2, 4),
    p_value = round(ct$p.value, 4)
  )
}

```

```{r}

# Run across all symbols except SPY (XLG included as diagnostic)
symbols_rel <- symbols_universe[symbols_universe != "SPY"]

lookback_results_rel <- map_dfr(symbols_rel, function(sym) {
  map_dfr(L_candidates, function(L) {
    compute_rel_cor(sym, L, H, train_df, spy_returns)
  })
})

```

```{r}

# Aggregate and select lookback_days from relative return analysis
lookback_summary_rel <- lookback_results_rel %>%
  group_by(L, H) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_n_obs       = round(mean(n_obs)),
    median_r        = round(median(r), 3),
    median_r_sq     = round(median(r_sq), 3),
    pct_significant = mean(p_value < 0.05),
    .groups         = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(L)

selected_L_rel <- lookback_summary_rel %>%
  filter(pct_significant >= 0.5) %>%
  arrange(desc(median_r), L) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  pull(L)

if (length(selected_L_rel) == 0) {
  selected_L_rel <- lookback_summary_rel %>%
    arrange(desc(pct_significant), desc(median_r)) %>%
    slice(1) %>%
    pull(L)
  warning("No relative-return candidate cleared the 50% threshold — selected by highest pct_significant.")
}

# Lock lookback_days from relative return analysis (primary selection basis)
lookback_days <- selected_L_rel
cat("Selected lookback_days (relative return):", lookback_days, "\n")

```

```{r}

# Summary table — relative return vs SPY
lookback_summary_rel %>%
  mutate(selected = if_else(L == lookback_days, "Yes", "")) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title    = "Lookback Period Selection — Relative Return vs SPY",
    subtitle = "Non-overlapping correlation: excess return signal vs 21-day forward excess return | Training data only"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    L               = "Lookback (days)",
    H               = "Holding (days)",
    avg_n_obs       = "Avg Samples",
    median_r        = "Median r",
    median_r_sq     = "Median r²",
    pct_significant = "% ETFs Significant",
    selected        = "Selected"
  ) %>%
  fmt_percent(columns = pct_significant, decimals = 0) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(median_r, median_r_sq), decimals = 3) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = list(cell_fill(color = col_navy), cell_text(color = "white", weight = "bold")),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_fill(color = col_band),
    locations = cells_body(rows = c(1, 3))
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = list(cell_fill(color = col_light_slate), cell_text(weight = "bold")),
    locations = cells_body(rows = selected == "Yes")
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(color = col_navy, weight = "bold", size = "large"),
    locations = cells_title(groups = "title")
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(color = col_slate, size = "small"),
    locations = cells_title(groups = "subtitle")
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.border.top.color            = col_navy,
    table.border.bottom.color         = col_navy,
    column_labels.border.bottom.color = col_navy,
    table.font.size                   = 14,
    data_row.padding                  = px(6)
  )

```

```{r}

# Detail table — relative return, one row per ETF x L, ordered by L then symbol
# Fixed height showing ~6-7 rows; remainder scrollable
lookback_results_rel %>%
  arrange(L, symbol) %>%
  mutate(significant = p_value < 0.05) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title    = "Lookback Correlation (Relative) — Full Detail",
    subtitle = "One row per ETF × lookback candidate | Excess return vs SPY | Training data only"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    symbol      = "ETF",
    L           = "Lookback (days)",
    H           = "Holding (days)",
    n_obs       = "Samples",
    r           = "r",
    r_sq        = "r²",
    p_value     = "p-value",
    significant = "p < 0.05"
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(r, r_sq, p_value), decimals = 3) %>%
  cols_hide(significant) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = list(cell_fill(color = col_navy), cell_text(color = "white", weight = "bold")),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_fill(color = col_light_slate),
    locations = cells_body(rows = significant == TRUE)
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(color = col_navy, weight = "bold", size = "large"),
    locations = cells_title(groups = "title")
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(color = col_slate, size = "small"),
    locations = cells_title(groups = "subtitle")
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.border.top.color            = col_navy,
    table.border.bottom.color         = col_navy,
    column_labels.border.bottom.color = col_navy,
    table.font.size                   = 14,
    data_row.padding                  = px(6),
    container.height                  = px(220),
    container.overflow.y              = "auto"
  )

```
